name: Legal Redline Summary - MSA v4.2.pdf
text:
[Page 1]
Meridian Holdings  Frontend Demo Grounded Corpus
LEG-001  Legal Redline Summary
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 1
DOCUMENT CONTROL
Legal Redline Summary — MSA v4.2
Negotiation memo showing partial movement by the vendor and unresolved residual risk.
Document ID
LEG-001
Prepared By
James Park, Privacy Counsel
Prepared For
Maria Santos, VP Procurement
Meeting
Governance review — 2024-03-15
Status
Open / partial vendor movement
Synthetic corporate document for demo, evaluation, and retrieval workflows only. All parties, people, systems, repositories, and events are
fictional.
Summary
This memo captures Meridian’s negotiation position against Master Service Agreement v4.2. The vendor moved on
several commercial points after the first review cycle, but the package remains unsuitable for execution without additional
changes.
Clause-by-Clause Redline Status
Clause Area
Vendor Draft 4.2
Meridian Baseline
Latest Vendor Reply
Residual
Risk
Liability Cap
Unlimited liability for both
parties.
General cap at 2×
annual value.
Vendor proposed cap at 12
months of fees paid;
Meridian position not
accepted.
Critical
Termination
Notice
90 days before
auto-renewal.
30 days before renewal.
Vendor accepted 30-day
notice for future drafts.
Low
Derivative IP
Joint ownership of
derivatives and
improvements.
Customer retains
derivative IP.
Vendor accepted Meridian
language, subject to final
scrub.
Low
Cross-Border
Transfers
No residency commitment;
SCCs deferred.
EEA residency or SCC
Appendix C.
Vendor agreed to attach
SCC Appendix C; draft not
yet executed.
Medium
Incident Notice
Commercially reasonable
efforts only.
24 hours for critical
customer-data incidents.
Vendor countered with 72
hours after confirmation.
High
Hard-case behavior: the vendor accepted some improvements, but the liability and incident-notification
positions remain materially weaker than Meridian’s baseline. This document should not be treated as a
clean approval.
Recommendation
●
Do not execute the package until liability is capped and incident-notification timing is clarified.

[Page 2]
Meridian Holdings  Frontend Demo Grounded Corpus
LEG-001  Legal Redline Summary
Controlled synthetic enterprise document — for retrieval, review, and workflow testing.
Page 2
●
If business timing requires pilot launch, isolate the launch under a written risk acceptance tied only to uptime during the
pilot window; do not waive liability or transfer-mechanism gaps.
●
Use this document together with the approved baseline and the current SLA when generating comparison decks or
executive recommendations.
